problems.title: Channel Names and Subscribers
problems.description: Write a SQL query to display the channel_name and subscriber_count for all channels.
problems.hint: Use a simple SELECT with column names from one table.
problems.solution: SELECT channel_name, subscriber_count FROM channels;
problems.explanation: The query selects only the requested columns from the channels table. SELECT specifies which columns to retrieve, FROM specifies the table.
problems.schema: CREATE TABLE channels (channel_id VARCHAR PRIMARY KEY, channel_name VARCHAR, subscriber_count BIGINT, view_count BIGINT, country VARCHAR, subscriber_hidden BOOLEAN, default_language VARCHAR, published_at DATE, video_count INT);

problems.title: First 10 Videos by Publish Date
problems.description: Write a query to display the video_id, title, and publish_date for the first 10 most recently published videos.
problems.hint: Use ORDER BY with DESC on publish_date, then LIMIT 10.
problems.solution: SELECT video_id, title, publish_date FROM videos ORDER BY publish_date DESC LIMIT 10;
problems.explanation: ORDER BY publish_date DESC sorts newest first. LIMIT 10 returns only the first 10 rows.

problems.description: Write a query to count the total number of channels in the database.
problems.hint: Use COUNT(*) to count all rows.
problems.solution: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_channels FROM channels;
problems.explanation: COUNT(*) counts all rows in the table. The AS keyword creates an alias for the output column.

problems.title: Rank Channels by Total Video Views
problems.description: Rank channels based on total video views using a window function.
problems.hint: Use CTE with SUM + RANK() window function.
problems.solution: WITH channel_views AS (SELECT c.channel_name, SUM(v.views) AS total_views FROM videos v JOIN channels c ON v.channel_id = c.channel_id GROUP BY c.channel_name) SELECT channel_name, total_views, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY total_views DESC) AS rank FROM channel_views;
problems.explanation: CTEs (WITH) create temporary named result sets. RANK() assigns a rank to each row.

problems.title: Top 3 Most Viewed Videos Within Each Channel
problems.description: For each channel, find the 3 most viewed videos.
problems.hint: Use CTE with ROW_NUMBER() partitioned by channel.
problems.solution: WITH ranked_videos AS (SELECT c.channel_name, v.title, v.views, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY v.channel_id ORDER BY v.views DESC) AS rn FROM videos v JOIN channels c ON v.channel_id = c.channel_id) SELECT channel_name, title, views FROM ranked_videos WHERE rn <= 3;
problems.explanation: ROW_NUMBER() with PARTITION BY resets the count for each channel.

problems.title: Monthly Upload Trend by Channel
problems.description: Show how many videos each channel uploaded per month.
problems.hint: Use strftime + GROUP BY on channel and month.
problems.solution: SELECT c.channel_name, strftime('%Y-%m', v.publish_date) AS month, COUNT(*) AS video_count FROM videos v JOIN channels c ON v.channel_id = c.channel_id GROUP BY c.channel_name, month ORDER BY c.channel_name, month;
problems.explanation: Extract year-month from dates to group by monthly periods.

problems.title: Month with Highest Upload Volume
problems.description: Find which month had the highest total video uploads across all channels.
problems.hint: Use CTE with GROUP BY + ORDER BY + LIMIT 1.
problems.solution: WITH monthly_counts AS (SELECT strftime('%Y-%m', publish_date) AS month, COUNT(*) AS upload_count FROM videos GROUP BY month) SELECT month, upload_count FROM monthly_counts ORDER BY upload_count DESC LIMIT 1;
problems.explanation: CTEs make complex queries readable. Combine with aggregation for clear insights.

problems.title: Latest Video Performing Above Channel Average
problems.description: Find the most recent video from each channel that has above-average views for that channel.
problems.hint: Use CTE + Window Function + JOIN — find latest video per channel that exceeds channel avg.
problems.solution: WITH channel_avg AS (SELECT channel_id, AVG(views) AS avg_views FROM videos GROUP BY channel_id), ranked_videos AS (SELECT v.channel_id, v.title, v.views, v.publish_date, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY v.channel_id ORDER BY v.publish_date DESC) AS rn FROM videos v JOIN channel_avg ca ON v.channel_id = ca.channel_id WHERE v.views > ca.avg_views) SELECT c.channel_name, rv.title, rv.views, rv.publish_date FROM ranked_videos rv JOIN channels c ON rv.channel_id = c.channel_id WHERE rv.rn = 1;
problems.explanation: Combine CTEs, window functions, and JOINs for advanced analytics.
